food_subgroup: Vegetable curry / legume and tuber dish

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Aloo Mutter; FOODON_03311737 (potato dish) is the closest parent category covering the primary ingredient.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (potato-based dishes) is the closest defensible parent; no specific Aloo Mutter entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Aloo Mutter or a sufficiently close Indian vegetable curry preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Aloo Mutter is approximately 250 g, equivalent to a standard restaurant or home-cooked bowl containing potatoes, green peas, and tomato-based curry sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Aloo Matar (Potato and Pea Curry) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) provides values for potato-based vegetable curries and cooked green peas. Per-100g values for Aloo Matar are derived by combining ICMR-NIN data for cooked potato curry and cooked green peas in a tomato-onion gravy, weighted approximately 55% potato, 20% peas, 25% gravy/sauce. Energy cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entries for comparable potato-pea curry preparations (USDA FDC ID 2103689 and related entries). Values represent a typical home-style preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — potato curry / mixed vegetable curry with legumes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Aloo Mutter in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 is estimated from the closest analogous entries: boiled potato (GI ~72 per Sydney GI Database) moderated by the presence of green peas (GI ~22, Sydney GI Database), tomato-based sauce, and dietary fibre content. Mixed potato-legume curry preparations in the Sydney GI Database cluster in the GI 48–56 range. A composite GI of 52 is used as a defensible central estimate. GL per serving = (52 × 13.2 × 2.5) / 100 = 17.2 for a 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from added salt and spice blends
hypertension.guidance: Aloo Mutter contains approximately 280 mg sodium per 100 g (700 mg per standard 250 g serving), which represents about 30% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size to 150 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and carbohydrate load from potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 52 and a glycemic load of 17 per 250 g serving, Aloo Mutter has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 150–180 g, pair with a high-fibre accompaniment such as whole-wheat roti, and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, plant-based ingredients, and dietary fibre from peas
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Aloo Mutter is a plant-based dish with low saturated fat (0.5 g per 100 g) and no cholesterol, and the green peas contribute soluble fibre that supports LDL cholesterol management; it is generally suitable for a heart-healthy diet when prepared with minimal added oil and salt.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aloo Mutter (also spelled Aloo Matar or Alu Matar) is a classic North Indian vegetarian curry made from diced potatoes (aloo) and green peas (mutter/matar) cooked in a spiced tomato-onion gravy. The dish is prepared by first sautéing onions, ginger, and garlic in oil, then adding tomatoes and a blend of ground spices including cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala to form a thick, aromatic sauce. Potatoes and peas are simmered in this gravy until tender. The texture is soft and hearty, with the potatoes absorbing the spiced sauce and the peas providing a slight sweetness and bite. The flavour profile is warm, mildly spiced, and savoury with a tangy tomato base. Aloo Mutter is a staple of everyday Indian home cooking and is widely served in Indian restaurants across Singapore and Southeast Asia. It is fully vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and halal. Nutritionally, it provides moderate carbohydrate from potato, plant protein from peas, dietary fibre, vitamin C, potassium, and iron. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI), moderated by the fibre and legume content. Sodium content is moderate and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension. It is commonly served with roti, naan, rice, or paratha.
common_user_queries: Is aloo matar good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for comparable Indian potato-pea curry preparations and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central. No single exact entry for Aloo Mutter exists in ICMR-NIN; values are derived by weighted combination of component data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using closest matching potato-legume curry entries. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this dish category. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN plant-based food entries. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as no vitamin D-contributing ingredients are present and ICMR-NIN reports negligible vitamin D for this dish type. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as no hydrogenated fats are used in standard preparation. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a fully plant-based dish. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use. Sodium value of 280 mg per 100 g reflects a typical restaurant preparation with added salt; home preparations may vary. The field survey data listing at Anjana Kitchen (Six Battery Road) at SGD 19.50 is noted as the commercial context for this record.
